commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1e521fcea8a9b0b05cf2cd6bddb1e03cc2a3ece4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay` and threads it through core and Codex app-server relay selection so loop-detection-only PreToolUse hook installation can be opted out.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +52. Total +62 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main and `v2026.6.10` still install synchronous Codex PreToolUse relay command hooks when loop detection is not explicitly false, and upstream Codex runs matched PreToolUse hooks as subprocesses before tool execution. I did not establish a live current-head load reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer API/config direction; repair automation cannot clear those blockers for the contributor.

target.title: fix(harness): add Codex pre-tool loop relay opt-out
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a narrow opt-out for Codex Harness loop-detection-only native PreToolUse relay subprocesses:

```json5
{
  tools: {
    loopDetection: {
      nativePreToolUseRelay: false,
    },
  },
}
```

Default behavior is preserved. If the new field is unset, Codex Harness keeps the existing native PreToolUse relay behavior unless `tools.loopDetection.enabled` is explicitly `false`.

## What Problem This Solves

Codex Harness bridges Codex native hook callbacks into OpenClaw through command hooks. For PreToolUse, that can install an `openclaw hooks relay` command hook before every tool call so OpenClaw can run before-tool policy / loop-detection checks.

On tool-heavy Codex Harness runs, the loop-detection-only path creates repeated short-lived OpenClaw CLI subprocesses. Codex ACP does not use this same native-hook relay path.

The intended fix is not to globally disable loop detection or change default behavior. It gives harness users one explicit switch, `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay=false`, to skip the loop-detection-only native PreToolUse relay while preserving other policy and trusted-tool paths.

## Changes

The PR keeps the scope intentionally small:

- Adds `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay?: boolean` to config parsing and config help metadata.
- Makes the harness relay predicate return false for loop-detection-only PreToolUse work when `nativePreToolUseRelay=false`.
- Threads that decision into the Codex app-server overlay so inactive PreToolUse becomes `hooks.PreToolUse=[]` instead of an `openclaw hooks relay --pre-tool-use-unavailable noop` command hook.
- Preserves explicit `before_tool_call` / trusted policy relay behavior.

No docs pages are changed in this PR; the public surface is limited to schema/help metadata plus runtime behavior and tests.

## Config behavior

- `nativePreToolUseRelay=false`: disables loop-detection-only native PreToolUse relay subprocess installation.
- unset or `true`: preserves current behavior.
- `tools.loopDetection.enabled=false`: still disables broader loop-detection behavior, but this PR does not require that broader switch.

## Evidence

Current PR head before the lint-only follow-up: `3a09080f`.

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts` -> 4 files passed, 228 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- CI failure investigation: `check-lint` only flagged one style issue in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` (`no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare`); the follow-up keeps the same behavior using `?? true`.

Regression coverage includes both layers that matter:

- Harness relay: `nativePreToolUseRelay=false` makes `shouldRelayEvent("pre_tool_use")` false.
- Codex app-server overlay: when inactive PreToolUse hook installation is disabled, generated config contains `hooks.PreToolUse=[]` rather than a no-op relay command hook.
target.author: marchpure

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
